sections.paragraph:
κλεῖθρον, ου, τό (s. κλείω and next entry; X., Pla. et al.; Sb 6253, 9 [137 b.c.]; PGM 4, 2261; 2294; LXX; ApcEsdr 5:13 p. 30, 12 Tdf. τὰ κλ. τοῦ πυλῶνος τῆς γυναικός; SibOr 2, 228) lit. a bar or bolt for closing a door; fig., a barrier, of the coast as a barrier for the sea 1 Cl 20:6 (Appian, Mithr. 24 §96 of the ‘bars’ with which an endangered seaport was closed).—B. 467. DELG s.v. κλείς.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 546
